scenes.text: Introduction to Docker: Docker is an open platform for developing, shipping, and running applications. It enables you to separate applications from your infrastructure so you can deliver software quickly. With Docker, you can manage your infrastructure in the same ways you manage your applications. By taking advantage of Docker’s methodologies for shipping, testing, and deploying code quickly, you can significantly reduce the delay between writing code and running it in production. Our lecture will cover Docker's architecture, key components, and how it enables efficient containerization.
scenes.image_prompt: graph LR; A(Docker Architecture) --> B{Components}; B --> C[Docker Client]; B --> D[Docker Daemon]; B --> E[Docker Images]; B --> F[Docker Containers];
